subject: [RFC PATCH 05/40] mm: page_alloc: remove watermark boost mechanism

reviews.author: Rik Riel (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged that the patch is no longer needed and decided to drop it due to extra code churn, but not before mentioning potential harm caused by watermark boosting.
reviews.raw_body:
I'll just drop this patch for now.

While we no longer need it, and the watermark boosting
can cause a little bit of harm at times, this probably
isn't worth the extra code churn in this series.

-- 
All Rights Reversed.

2026-05-27.patch_summary: This patch removes the watermark boost mechanism from the Linux kernel's page allocation code. The mechanism was introduced to react to fragmentation events at the pageblock granularity, but it has been found to cause issues in workloads with high memory availability. With the introduction of superpageblocks and anti-fragmentation enforcement at 1 GiB granularity, sub-pageblock fallbacks are no longer meaningful signals. The boost is also applied before the success path is decided, leading to unnecessary wakeups of kswapd even when free pages are abundant. The patch drops the entire mechanism, removing various functions, flags, and sysctls related to watermark boosting.
